Services are restored.

From Zoom:
Services have been restored, if you are still having connection issues please flush your DNS cache and attempt to reconnect.

For Windows open a command prompt and type "ipconfig /flushdns"
For Mac open a terminal window and type "sudo dscacheutil -flushcache; sudo killall -HUP mDNSResponder"

title: Jamf: Threat detection notifications for App Store flagged as a sideloaded app
summary:
This incident has been resolved. 

Actions: A fix has been applied for notifications of App Store app flagging as sideloaded.

Customer Action Required: UEM Synchronization
To remediate the side-loaded app events identified during this incident, please perform a UEM sync between Jamf Security Cloud and your MDM solution. This sync will also update device risk state used by Access policies.
To synchronize: Navigate to Jamf Security Cloud > Integrations > UEM Connection > Settings > Actions > Sync

title: Jamf: Jamf School Outage - eu-central-1
summary:
**Incident Date:** September 24, 2025

**Duration:** 15 minutes

**Impact:** Temporary unavailability of Jamf School services in eu-central-1

**Summary and Timeline**

On September 17th, Jamf School experienced a performance degradation. To remediate and prevent recurrence, we scheduled a schema change to add a supporting index to high-concurrency table. When executed, the index creation acquired an unexpected lock that blocked concurrent application queries, resulting in a full Jamf School ser

title: Jamf: Jamf Protect Reporting and Forwarding Degradation
summary:
Between Wed, Sept 10, 22:05 UTC and Fri, Sept 19, 22:05 UTC, customers may have experienced unreported service degradation for Jamf Protect's reporting and forwarding capabilities via JamfCloud. The impacts to the degraded service were not fully understood until now. We're improving monitoring to better detect and surface hidden impacts going forward. 

During this time window, affected customers may have experienced a lower volume of reported events and telemetry. On-device protections includin

title: Jamf: JCDS Outage
summary:
**Detailed Root Cause Analysis Report: JCDS Outage**  

**Incident Date:** July 17, 2025 

**Duration:** 29 minutes   

**Impact:**  Global disruption of Jamf Cloud Distribution Service \(JCDS\) 

**Summary and Timeline** 

On July 17, 2025, a JCDS outage impacted customers globally when a parameter value did not update, blocking content distribution to end-user devices. Jamf restored full service within 29 minutes. 

**Root Cause & Timeline**

title: Jamf: Jamf Security Cloud - secure DNS services are not working due to a wrong certificate
summary:
**June 15, 2025 - Jamf SecureDNS Outage RCA**  
  
**Summary and Timeline**    
On June 15, 2025 \(00:00 UTC\), devices using SecureDNS lost internet access due to an outage affecting Web Threat Prevention and Content Filtering capabilities within Jamf Safe Internet, Jamf Protect, and Jamf Trust services.The root cause of the incident was an expired SSL certificate, leading to loss of security features or loss of internet connectivity for end user devices. Although the certificate in question wa

title: Jamf: Jamf Pro Outage
summary:
**Root Cause Analysis of Jamf Pro Outage**  
During scheduled maintenance on a low-level component of the Jamf Pro cloud infrastructure, an update failed, resulting in an outage that affected Jamf Pro customers. The component in question is a critical part of our auto-scaling infrastructure, which enables Jamf to support millions of devices. The failure of this upgrade led to a widespread outage.

* The update was not backwards-compatible, making it impossible to roll back to the previous versio

title: Jamf: Infrastructure Misconfiguration Related to Security Groups Governing the Access to our Database Subsystem for Jamf Security Cloud
summary:
This impacted the Jamf Security Cloud administration interface to not be available, the application of Jamf Security policies to not be functional, and the device status update processing was slowed down / paused.

We have resolved this and Jamf Security Cloud is working as expected.

title: Kuali Build: Platform/Build Brief Interruption - Canada
summary: Our engineers were unable to identify a specific root cause. However, the issue has been resolved after restarting the Identity service and the Canda instance. We will continue to monitor the system to ensure stability.

title: Kuali Build: Platform/Build - Apps not loading
summary: We've resolved the issue and apps are loading as expected. We apologize for the inconvenience and are taking steps to prevent similar issues in the future.

title: Kuali Build: Platform/Build - Sites not loading
summary: The service disruption that occurred today was due to an outage with one of our infrastructure providers, Fly.io. This impacted our ability to deploy services and resulted in temporary unavailability of our environments. The issue has been resolved, and all services are now operational and performing as expected. We are reviewing our deployment processes to further reduce the risk and impact of similar provider-related incidents in the future. Additional details are available on the Fly.io statu

title: Kuali Build: Platform/Build - Errors with integrations and variables in workflows
summary: Our team has resolved the issues causing the missing variables within workflows and errors with integrations on forms. We identified an error within a recent deploy that has been corrected.   We’ll be looking into ways to avoid this type of issue in the future and we apologize for the inconvenience this caused.  If you have any further questions please feel free to reach out to our support team.

title: Kuali Build: Intermittent Errors When Navigating Platform/Build
summary: Some users may have experienced intermittent errors when accessing pages due to a server entering an unhealthy state. Once identified, our engineers eliminated the unhealthy node and brought a healthy server online to stabilize the environment. During the transition, some customers may have noticed temporary slowness, particularly with actions such as PDF exports or workflow executions. The migration was completed successfully, and the system has since returned to normal operation.

title: Kuali Build: Platform/Build - Issues with submissions going to error
summary: After investigation we found this incident was caused by networking issues with one of our service providers \(Fly.io\). We are looking into adding additional alerts when our system jobs get into a bad state and long term infrastructure improvements that are more robust to help avoid in the future. We apologize for the inconvenience caused by this issue.

title: Kuali Build: Platform/Build - Issues with submissions going to error
summary: After investigation we found this incident was caused by networking issues with one of our service providers \(Fly.io\). We are looking into adding additional alerts when our system jobs get into a bad state and long term infrastructure improvements that are more robust to help avoid in the future. We apologize for the inconvenience caused by this issue.

title: Kuali Build: Platform/Build slowness
summary:
We discovered long running queries in our codebase that was causing our database to get into a bad state. We’ve improved the query performance by adding additional indexes so results are returned much more efficiently. We’ve also streamlined our process when adding new queries to maintain performance in the future. Lastly, we’re also looking into long term infrastructure improvements that can better distribute performance spikes.

We apologize for the inconvenience and we appreciate your patienc

title: Kuali Build: Build/Platform not loading
summary: The issue was caused by increased load on the database, which temporarily impacted application availability. We’re making performance improvements to help prevent future incidents.
